body.para: On the DS10 target the storage path is short and fixed: the EV6 reaches the PCI hose through the Pchip, the Cypress CY82C693 sits on PCI bus 0 (slot 5) as the PCI-to-ISA bridge and IDE controller, and the storage endpoints (the ATAPI CD-ROM and any ATA disk) hang off the CY82C693 IDE channel. There is no separate storage switch or expander -- the "fabric" is just the IDE channel and its master / slave units.

SCSI (the dka path) and the legacy DEC interconnects are reference material for other platforms; on the DS10 they are not part of the active fabric. See SCSI Devices and the legacy-bus page for those.
